series_description:
Records relating to all onsite accidents,
including accident reports, supplemental
accident reports, photographs,
correspondence, and related
documentation.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 5 years
comments:
Accident or occupational disease
reports (by supervisors and
employees) and other associated
reports required to be submitted to
the State Office of Risk
Management (SORM) through the
Risk Management Information
System (RMIS).

retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Claims; invoices; statements; copies of
checks and purchase orders; expenditure
authorizations; ledgers; encumbrance,
purchase, general journal, expenditure,
and special vouchers; and similar records
that serve to document disbursements,
including those documenting claims for
and reimbursement to employees for
travel and other expenses.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing
registers, account cards, deposit warrants,
cash receipts, credit card receipts, receipts
books, cash transfers, daily cash reports,
reconciliations, general journal vouchers,
special vouchers, and similar records that
serve to document money owed to or
received by a state agency and its
collection or receipt.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Used to create employee leave balances,
to transfer leave balances when an
employee transfers positions, to adjust
carry-over balances on August 31, to
correct errors on leave accumulation, and
to close out leave accounts on separated
employees.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Self evaluations and plans documenting
compliance with the requirements of
Americans with Disabilities Act.
retention_statement: 3 years

series_description:
Transcripts and final decisions of hearings
conducted as part of the regulatory
process, and hearings on proposed rules
and changes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Only records that lead to
substantial or statutory changes to
the operations of the agency and
its policies and procedures should
be retained for archival review.
Contact the State Archives when
these records have met their
retention periods.
CAUTION: These records may be
maintained with related
information, including meeting
notices, proofs of publication, and
meeting minutes, and require a
longer retention period.This series
is not for any files related to C & E
cases; see C & E Investigative File
(AIN 26).

retention_statement: 5 years
comments:
Series includes records from
3.1.001a/b on the State RRS

series_description:
Fire, safety and other inspection records
of facilities and equipment.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
inspection reports of building
construction. SEE item number
5.2.028.

series_description:
Final agreement described by Government
Code 2009.054(c), associated with a
matter conducted under an alternative
dispute resolution procedure in which
personnel of a state agency participated
as a party on the agency's behalf.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
legal_citation:
Texas Civil Practice
and Remedies Code,
Chapter 154.071.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
Required by the General
Appropriations Act (100 Day
Report).
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Annual Financial Reports must
be submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(a). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Required by the General Appropriations
Act. Includes encumbrances and
documentation about budget transfers
and revisions, as well as detail charts of
accounts.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
Required by the General
Appropriations Act.

series_description: Building and surrounding property.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Aptitude or skills tests required of job
applicants or of current personnel to
qualify for promotion or transfer.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: One copy of each
different test (different in terms of
either questions or administration
procedures) should be retained for
the period indicated.

series_description:
Audits and reviews performed by or on
behalf of an agency, including the working
papers that support the audit. Also
includes audits performed on the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
The State Auditor's Office (SAO)
retains any copies of its audits
performed on Texas state agencies.

series_description:
Notes taken during open meetings from
which written minutes are prepared.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days

series_title:
Board Meetings, Agendas
and Minutes or Audiovisual
Recordings of Closed Session
series_description:
Agendas, minutes, or audiovisual
recordings of closed meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: This must be used for
those state boards, committees,
councils, and commissions which
by law or the biennial
Appropriations Act, are
administered by another state
agency. These records and all
others related to the functions of
any of these dependent entities
must be included in the records
retention schedule of the
administering agency.

series_title:
Board Meeting Agenda &
Minutes Open Session
series_description:
Agendas and minutes of state boards,
committees, commissions, and councils
that conduct open meetings as required
by Chapter 551, Government Code.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
Agency retains permanent record
copy. The archival requirement will
be met by sending a copy to the
Archives and Information Services
Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.

series_title:
Board Meeting Audio and
Video Recordings for Minutes
series_description:
Audio or videotapes of open meetings of
state board, commissions committees,
and councils and councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days

series_description:
Documents submitted at meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils including exhibit items,
documentation for agenda items, etc.
Includes documents sent in advance of
meetings for briefing purposes, some of
which may not be submitted at an actual
meeting.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
Documents are retained for five
years as opposed to the standard
two years as a reference tool for
future meetings.

series_description:
Project records related to planning,
design, construction, conversion, or
modernization of state facilities,
structures, infrastructure, and systems,
including feasibility, screening, and
implementation studies; topographical
and soil surveys and reports; as-builts;
laboratory test reports; environmental
impact statements; correspondence;
successful and unsuccessful bid
documentation and other related
documentation.
retention_statement: 10 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Archival review
designation is for state-owned
buildings only.

series_description:
files in which are on hold past the
standard retention schedule
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
Retained as long as files exist which
are listed. C & E
refers to Compliance and
Enforcement.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years
comments:
Paper originals are destroyed after
scanning and quality control
review.

series_title:
C & E Orders – Consent
Orders, Final Orders, Agreed
Board Orders, Order of the
Board, Cease and Desist
Orders, Agreements,
Injunctions, Default
Judgments
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
Scanned for disaster recovery
purposes although originals are
retained. Documents with original
signature must be kept.

retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
If we receive hard copy, these are
scanned into the document
management system and the hard
copy is destroyed.
If we receive an electronic version
this is put directly into the
document management system.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
If we receive hard copy, these are
scanned into the document
management system and the hard
copy is destroyed. If we receive an
electronic version this is put
directly into the document
management system.

series_description:
Calendars, appointment books or
programs and scheduling, or itinerary
records, purchased with state funds or
maintained by staff during business hours
that document appointments, itineraries,
and other activities of agency officials or
employees.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year
comments:
If we receive an electronic version
this is put directly into the
document management system.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected
officials, executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors, and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds, but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period.
See Open Records Decision 635
issued in December 1995 by the
Attorney General.

series_description:
Files and reports from electronic database
audits to select licensees or registrants for
continuing education audit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
Paper or electronic documents are
requested upon audit. If we receive
hard copy, these are scanned into
document management system
and the hard copy is destroyed.

series_description:
Notice to license holder of audit,
responsive information from license
holder, and associated correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments: CEP refers to Continuing Education

series_description:
Schedules of prices charged by an agency
for services to the public or other agencies
including any documentation used to
determine the charges.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_title:
CHRC Fingerprint cards and
DPS records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CHRC refers to Criminal History
Record Check.
These paper cards are erroneously
submitted by license holders for
purpose of criminal history check.

series_description:
Contracts, leases, and agreements include
general obligation, land lease, utilities, and
construction except for buildings.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
Series includes records from
5.1.001a/b on the State RRS.

series_description:
Incoming/outgoing and internal
correspondence pertaining to the
formulation, planning, implementation,
interpretation, modification, or
redefinition of the programs, services, or
projects of an agency and the
administrative regulations, policies, and
procedures that govern them.
retention_statement: 4 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
administrative correspondence of
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: This records series and
item number 1.1.008 should be
used only for correspondence that
is not included in or directly related
to another records series on the
agency's approved schedule.

series_description:
Non-administrative incoming/outgoing
and internal correspondence, in any
media, pertaining to or arising from the
routine operations of the policies,
programs, services, or projects of an
agency.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_description:
Any record created by an agency to track
any type of incoming or outgoing
correspondence or packages by the U.S.
Postal Service or by private couriers.
retention_statement: 1 year

series_description:
Criminal history record information on job
applicants or agency employees obtained
from the Department of Public Safety
(DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain a criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agency's
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information.

series_description:
Surveys soliciting feedback from
customers or clients of an agency, and the
statistical data maintained rating an
agency's performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
SEE item number 1.1.067 for
summary reports compiled from
customer surveys.

series_description:
Plans developed (per Texas Labor Code,
412.054) to mitigate the damage of
potential events that could endanger an
agency’s ability to function.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
3 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Documentation used to start,
modify, or top all voluntary or
required deductions from payroll,
including garnishment or other court-
ordered attachments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Disciplinary actions are those actions
which may affect pay, status, or tenure.
They are imposed to discipline an
employee whose conduct is harmful to the
best interests of the state, the agency, or
the employee work force; and for failure
to improve performance or conduct
following imposition of corrective action.
May include cause for demotion,
suspension, or dismissal, and the reasons
for failure to give written notice of
resignation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
May also be used to document
evidence of employee self-
improvement efforts, as well as
favorable and unfavorable
communication.

retention_statement: 4 years

series_description:
Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax
records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Records relating to the review of
employee grievances against personnel
policies, working conditions, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an agency
employee with the Equal
Employment Office(EEO).

series_description:
Forms or similar records used to create or
change information concerning the
records of an employee, including pay
grade, position classification, employee
number, evaluation date, and termination

series_description:
Agency copies of information relating to

disability, health, and other types of

its employees.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Documents that serve
as payroll deduction authorizations
must be maintained for the
retention period prescribed for
item number 3.2.001.

retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
Until Superseded
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts
showing the overall arrangement
and administrative structure of the
state agency need to be transferred
to the Archives and Information
Services Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
Organizational charts showing
division /department level detail
are not considered archival.

retention_statement: 2 years

series_description:
Any internally distributed manuals,
guidelines, or similar records that define
agency wide policies and procedures.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Job descriptions, including all associated
task or skill statements for positions.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Any reports compiled by an agency on
aspects of personnel staffing.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years
4 years

Employee Time Off and Sick
Leave

Employee Training and
Educational Achievements
Records
series_description:
Certificates of completion, transcripts, test
scores, or similar records documenting the
training, testing, or continuing education
achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Employee Verification
Records for Former
Employees
series_description:
Minimum information needed to verify

Fiscal Year End plus 4 years

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents
whose submission by candidates for
vacant positions is required on the
application form, by application
procedures, or in the employment
advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description: Federal reporting form
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Federal regulation
requires that INS 1-9 forms be
retained for 3 years from date of
hire or 1 year after separation of
the employee, whichever later.
Agencies should make certain that
the INS I-9 forms for employees
who terminate from an agency less
than 3 years from date of hire are
kept for the 3 year retention
period.

series_description:
Internal or external announcements or

promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.

series_description:

5.1001.

retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

Forms and logs used to reconcile

comments:
Includes all documents pertaining

series_description:
From agency legal counsel or the Attorney
General, including any requests eliciting
the opinions.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Opinions and
advice that set legal precedent or
exhibit historical value will be
evaluated by the Archives and
Information Services Division of the
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission for archival
preservation. CAUTION: Does not
include legal opinions or advice
rendered on a matter in litigation
or with regard to pending litigation.
See item number 1.1.048.

series_description:
Subpoenas for production of evidence

agency is not a party.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
For subpoenas related to litigation
in which the state agency is a party,
see AIN 130.

series_description:
Including any supporting documentation

The final version of Legislative
Appropriation Requests must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

Records management officers must

2 years

series_description:
Any records of an agency needed for the
documentation of output, outcome,
efficiency, and explanatory measures in an
agency's appropriations request or
strategic plan, and for performance
measures used to manage the agency.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
The FE + 3 retention period
overrides any shorter retention
period for a records series in this
schedule if the records series is
needed for documentation of
agency performance measures.

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the process
of planning new or redefined programs,
services, or projects of an agency that are
not included in or directly related to other
records series in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Drafts of proposed legislation and
related correspondence.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
News, press releases, or any public
relations files maintained or issued by an
agency. Includes print, audio, and
audiovisual records.
retention_statement: 2 years

series_description:
Background material, copy (drafts),
original artwork, photo negatives, prints,
flats, etc. This includes all work
performed both inside and outside the
agency.”
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Successive and
substantive drafts of major
publications may be archival. Major
is defined by both the publication's
authorship and its impact on Texas
and Texans. Original artwork,
including photo prints and
negatives that have significant
value as evidence of agency
programs as well as the potential
for re-use, may be archival.

series_description:
Includes bid requisition or authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid
specifications, successful bids, and bid
tabulation and evaluation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Unsuccessful bids that do not meet agency
submission requirements and are not
included in a bid evaluation process (e.g.
withdraw, missed submission deadline,
incomplete submission, etc.).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Requests for information preliminary to
the procurement of goods or service by
direct purchase or bid.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
Caution: If the request for
information leads to request for
proposal or bid, the request for
information documentation should
be retained in accordance with
item number 5.3.007.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
For instructions regarding the
determination of the closure of
accounts and for additional
information regarding the retention
period see the most current edition
of the Benefits Coordinator
Manual.

series_description:
Includes documentation of certification
and approval - forms SLR 105C (formerly
RMD 105C), and/or other forms
designated by the State Records
Administrator.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
Original is retained permanently by
the State & Local Records
Management Division, Texas State
Library & Archives Commission.

series_description:
Forms indicate records transferred to
storage or a transfer of legal custody.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Biennial narrative reports to the governor
and legislature as required by an agency's
enabling statutes, including annual
narrative reports if they are required by
statute.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3. The requirement is
met by sending the required copies
of the reports to Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final report disposed
of at the expiration of the retention
period.

series_description:
Quarterly and annual reports on agency
performance measures submitted to
executive and legislative offices.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years

series_description:
Annual, sub-annual, or special reports or
studies on non-fiscal aspects of an
agency's programs, services, or projects
compiled by agency personnel, by advisory
committees, or by consultants under
contract with an agency that are not
noted elsewhere in this schedule. Includes
reports distributed either internally or to
other entities.
retention_statement: 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Reports are
archival when they deal with
significant aspects of the agency's
programs. For agencies that have
had an archival appraisal, separate
this records series by each type of
archival coding, A, R, or E. See page
ix for more information.

series_description:
Logs or similar records used to register
persons appearing before state agencies
as required by Chapter 2004, Government
Code, including quarterly reports filed
with the Texas Ethics Commission.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title:
Request for Authority to
Dispose of State Records
(RMD 102)
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
Original is retained permanently by
the State & Local Records
Management Division, Texas State
Library & Archives Commission.

series_description:
Rules, policies, and procedures that
govern an agency's programs,
services, or projects distributed
internally for the use of employees
or externally to the public.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies,
and procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description:
Records relating to the issuance of keys,
identification cards, building passes,
passwords, signed statements or similar
the instruments of access to agency or
state facilities, equipment or automated
systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Agency copy of forms completed by
mechanical service personnel for
installation or repair.
retention_statement: 1 year

series_description:
Signature cards or similar records
establishing authority of an agency
employee to initiate or authorize financial
transactions on behalf of an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Automated software applications
and operating system files including
job control language, program
listing/source code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all
technical characteristics necessary
for reading or processing of
electronic records and their timely,
authorized disposition
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered in
conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.

retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Strategic Plans must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Sunset Review Report, agency self-study,
and other correspondence and supporting
documentation related to the Sunset
review process for state agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Related
documentation includes the agency
self-evaluation report, agency
response to the commission's draft
report, and documentation of the
agency's implementation of the
commission's final
recommendations.

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn,
emergency, and adopted rules; open
meetings notices; or any other documents
required by law to be submitted to the
Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Reports to the agency from TWC or
its predecessor pertaining to employees.
retention_statement: 3 years

series_description:
Records of temporary usefulness that are
not an integral part of a records series of
an agency, that are not regularly filed
within an agency’s recordkeeping system,
and that are required only for a limited
period of time for the completion of an
action by an official or employee of the
agency or in the preparation of an ongoing
records series.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers should use caution in
assigning this records series item
number to records of an agency to
make certain they are not part of
another records series listed in this
schedule or, for records series
unique to an agency, are not part
of a records series that documents
the fulfillment of the statutory
obligations of the agency or the
documentation of its functions.

series_description:
Periodic reports compiled from
information entered into the Uniform
Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Logs, registers, or similar records
documenting visitors to limited access or
restricted areas of agency facilities.
retention_statement: 3 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 75 years
